args.query: Workday official security guidance never share verification code

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Workday Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup for Personal Devices

  - url: https://www.oregon.gov/das/workday/documents/workday-mfa-setup.pdf

  - source: Oregon Department of Administrative Services

  - domain: oregon.gov


  ## Content


  # Workday Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup for Personal Devices


  This job aid explains how users who are required to sign in to Workday with multi-factor
  authentication can begin MFA setup for Workday Native access. It is written for
  employees or contingent workers who do not use a state-issued Microsoft 365 account,
  or who need Workday access from a personal device.


  Before beginning, the user must know the Workday username and password and have
  a smart device nearby with an authentication application installed. The guide recommends
  Microsoft Authenticator, while noting that the user may already have another supported
  authenticator application.


  The guide distinguishes an in-account MFA setup flow from an access-recovery problem.
  A user who already has MFA configured but has received a new device must contact
  the appropriate Security Partner to have MFA reset. Users who encounter MFA reset
  or login problems are directed to their HR office for assistance.


  The setup flow also has attempt limits. The QR code can be used only a limited number
  of times during setup. After repeated unsuccessful attempts, the user may be temporarily
  locked out and must wait before trying again.


  ## Beginning the in-account verification flow


  1. Sign in to Workday using the normal Workday username and password.


  2. When Workday presents the **Extra Verification Required** screen, select **Set
  Up Now**.


  3. Workday then presents the **Set Up Authenticator App** screen. At this point,
  continue the setup process on the user''s smart device.


  The guide treats this as a continuation of an active Workday sign-in session. It
  does not describe ordinary MFA setup as a general external support submission. Separate
  reset or login-help routes apply when the user cannot continue with the existing
  sign-in or no longer has the previously configured authentication device.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Workday Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup for Personal Devices
url: https://www.oregon.gov/das/workday/documents/workday-mfa-setup.pdf

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Login Help

  - url: https://www.workday.com/en-us/signin.html

  - source: Workday

  - domain: workday.com


  ## Content


  # Login Help


  ## Need help accessing your account?


  Workday explains that employee accounts are accessed through each company''s unique
  Workday sign-in page. A user who needs the correct sign-in link should contact the
  employer''s HR or IT department.


  Questions about pay, taxes, timesheets, benefits, or job applications also belong
  with the user''s employer. Workday states that, because of its security policy,
  it cannot provide direct assistance with those employee-account requests.


  This means that a general Workday corporate login page is not a substitute for the
  user''s employer-specific tenant or authentication flow. When an employee has a
  real access or login problem, the appropriate help path is through the organization
  that manages that Workday account and its sign-in configuration.


  The page separately lists logins for Workday products and community services. Those
  product and community links are distinct from an employee''s employer-managed Workday
  account.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Login Help
url: https://www.workday.com/en-us/signin.html

args.query: authentication values verification challenge user must complete assistant cannot handle Workday security boundary

content.content:
The blockage is **account verification**, not a direct-deposit change.

Current status:

- **Workflow:** Confirm current direct-deposit destination
- **Configuration change requested:** No
- **Route:** Existing authenticated Workday session—not account recovery
- **Active step:** A challenge using your already registered authentication factor
- **Status:** Waiting for you to complete that challenge

To proceed safely:

1. Return to the employer-specific Workday sign-in/session.
2. Complete the prompted verification using your enrolled authenticator—such as approving the notification or entering the code **directly into Workday**.
3. Do **not** send me your password, one-time code, approval details, or recovery information.
4. After Workday reports verification as complete, return to the direct-deposit confirmation flow and confirm the existing destination. This should not change your bank details.

If you no longer have access to the enrolled device or the challenge fails, use your employer’s HR/IT or Workday security-support process for an MFA reset. Workday’s guidance says employee login and payroll-account issues are handled through the employer’s Workday support channel.

final_answer:
The blockage is **account verification**, not a direct-deposit change.

Current status:

- **Workflow:** Confirm current direct-deposit destination
- **Configuration change requested:** No
- **Route:** Existing authenticated Workday session—not account recovery
- **Active step:** A challenge using your already registered authentication factor
- **Status:** Waiting for you to complete that challenge

To proceed safely:

1. Return to the employer-specific Workday sign-in/session.
2. Complete the prompted verification using your enrolled authenticator—such as approving the notification or entering the code **directly into Workday**.
3. Do **not** send me your password, one-time code, approval details, or recovery information.
4. After Workday reports verification as complete, return to the direct-deposit confirmation flow and confirm the existing destination. This should not change your bank details.

If you no longer have access to the enrolled device or the challenge fails, use your employer’s HR/IT or Workday security-support process for an MFA reset. Workday’s guidance says employee login and payroll-account issues are handled through the employer’s Workday support channel.
